{{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Kindaichi Onsen|ja|金田一温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|ja|山口駅|12=draft}} }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=draft}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft}}). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=draft}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ|12=draft}} {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=draft}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=draft}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=draft}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}}, the hot spring town features large {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|12=draft}}, traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}}, and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=draft}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=draft}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省|12=draft}}|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=draft}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=draft}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>

The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=draft}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=draft}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Shūnan route)
=== By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft}}. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft}}, or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 262|ja|国道262号|12=draft}} and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}} can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Town|ja|温泉街|12=draft}}, {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=Onsen rankings|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Hot Spring Areas|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=draft}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=draft}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Kindaichi Onsen|ja|金田一温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|ja|山口駅|12=draft}} }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=draft}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft}}). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=draft}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ|12=draft}} {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=draft}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=draft}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=draft}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}}, the hot spring town features large {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|12=draft}}, traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}}, and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=draft}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=draft}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft}}|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=draft}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=draft}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|12=draft}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as {{ill|Inoue Kaoru|ja|井上馨|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahara Chuya|ja|中原中也|12=draft}}. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the {{ill|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum|ja|中原中也記念館|12=draft}}. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|12=draft}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet {{ill|Taneda Santoka|ja|種田山頭火|12=draft}} once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=draft}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=draft}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk.
=== Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft}}), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=draft}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=draft}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=draft}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Shūnan route)
=== By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft}}. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft}}, or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 262|ja|国道262号|12=draft}} and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}} can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Town|ja|温泉街|12=draft}}, {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=Onsen rankings|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Hot Spring Areas|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=draft}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=draft}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Kindaichi Onsen|ja|金田一温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|ja|山口駅|12=draft}} }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=draft}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft}}). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=draft}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ|12=draft}} {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=draft}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=draft}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=draft}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}}, the hot spring town features large {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|12=draft}}, traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}}, and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=draft}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=draft}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft}}|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=draft}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=draft}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|12=draft}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as {{ill|Inoue Kaoru|ja|井上馨|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahara Chuya|ja|中原中也|12=draft}}. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the {{ill|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum|ja|中原中也記念館|12=draft}}. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|12=draft}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet {{ill|Taneda Santoka|ja|種田山頭火|12=draft}} once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=draft}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=draft}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk.
=== Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft}}), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=draft}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=draft}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=draft}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft}} on the Shūnan route)
=== By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft}}. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft}}, or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 262|ja|国道262号|12=draft}} and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}} can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Town|ja|温泉街|12=draft}}, {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=Onsen rankings|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Hot Spring Areas|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=draft}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=draft}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Kindaichi Onsen|ja|金田一温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|ja|山口駅|12=draft|12=draft}} }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=draft|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=draft|12=draft}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft|12=draft}}). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=draft|12=draft}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=draft|12=draft}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=draft|12=draft}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the hot spring town features large {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|12=draft|12=draft}}, traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft|12=draft}}, and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft|12=draft}}, this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft|12=draft}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=draft|12=draft}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=draft|12=draft}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft|12=draft}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft|12=draft}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=draft|12=draft}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|12=draft|12=draft}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as {{ill|Inoue Kaoru|ja|井上馨|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahara Chuya|ja|中原中也|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the {{ill|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum|ja|中原中也記念館|12=draft|12=draft}}. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|12=draft|12=draft}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet {{ill|Taneda Santoka|ja|種田山頭火|12=draft|12=draft}} once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=draft|12=draft}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=draft|12=draft}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk.
=== Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft|12=draft}}), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=draft|12=draft}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=draft|12=draft}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=draft|12=draft}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Shūnan route)
=== By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft|12=draft}}, or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 262|ja|国道262号|12=draft|12=draft}} and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Town|ja|温泉街|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=Onsen rankings|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Hot Spring Areas|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=draft|12=draft}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Kindaichi Onsen|ja|金田一温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft|12=draft}}|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|ja|山口駅|12=draft|12=draft}} }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=draft|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=draft|12=draft}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft|12=draft}}). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=draft|12=draft}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=draft|12=draft}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=draft|12=draft}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the hot spring town features large {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|12=draft|12=draft}}, traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft|12=draft}}, and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft|12=draft}}, this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft|12=draft}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=draft|12=draft}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=draft|12=draft}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft|12=draft}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft|12=draft}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=draft|12=draft}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>

The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=draft|12=draft}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=draft|12=draft}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yuda Onsen Station|ja|湯田温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk.
=== Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft|12=draft}}), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=draft|12=draft}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=draft|12=draft}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=draft|12=draft}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Shūnan route)
=== By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 9|ja|国道9号|12=draft|12=draft}}, or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 262|ja|国道262号|12=draft|12=draft}} and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.

Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>
