{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]] | Location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station]] }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Shiyu|1=Bōchō Shiyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65291872}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * [[:en:Alkali|Alkaline]] {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source Water Temperature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansuien|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koroshi Isamu|1=Isamu Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643116}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], the hot spring town features large [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]], and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|1=Tsuwano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146437}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/restaurants|1=restaurant|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666766}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|1=Kōfu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228449}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|1=Tottori Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674516}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|1=Matsue-shinjiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531630}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Osamu|1=Michio Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585906}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]]|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|qq=}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|1=Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466617}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-yama|1=Gongen-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542316}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō†Jizō|qq=}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as [[:en:Inoue Kaoru|Inoue Kaoru]] and [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Nakahara Chuya]]. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the [[:en:Nakahara Chūya Memorial Museum|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum]]. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuraya|qq=}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet [[:en:Santōka Taneda|Taneda Santoka]] once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpo no Yado|1=Kanpo no Yado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264563}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}}’s [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]] at [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]], about a 10-minute walk. === Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|1=Yamaguchi Prefectural Road Route 204|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467194}} (formerly [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]]), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Chūgoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JRバス中国|1=Chugoku JR Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10874403}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagi Express|1=Hagi Express|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620283}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|1=Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592158}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Shūnan route) === By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|1=Yuda Onsen parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563751}} on the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]]. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogori Interchange|1=Kogori Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463845}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]], or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Interchange|1=Yamaguchi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11044713}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 262|National Route 262]] and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōfu Higashi Interchange|1=Hofu-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657010}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.

Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]] | Location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station]] }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Shiyu|1=Bōchō Shiyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65291872}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * [[:en:Alkali|Alkaline]] {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source Water Temperature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansuien|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koroshi Isamu|1=Isamu Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643116}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], the hot spring town features large [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]], and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|1=Tsuwano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146437}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/restaurants|1=restaurant|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666766}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|1=Kōfu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228449}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|1=Tottori Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674516}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|1=Matsue-shinjiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531630}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Osamu|1=Michio Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585906}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]]|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|qq=}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|1=Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466617}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-yama|1=Gongen-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542316}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō†Jizō|qq=}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as [[:en:Inoue Kaoru|Inoue Kaoru]] and [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Nakahara Chuya]]. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the [[:en:Nakahara Chūya Memorial Museum|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum]]. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuraya|qq=}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet [[:en:Santōka Taneda|Taneda Santoka]] once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpo no Yado|1=Kanpo no Yado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264563}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}}’s [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]] at [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]], about a 10-minute walk. === Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|1=Yamaguchi Prefectural Road Route 204|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467194}} (formerly [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]]), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Chūgoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JRバス中国|1=Chugoku JR Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10874403}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagi Express|1=Hagi Express|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620283}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|1=Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592158}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Shūnan route) === By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|1=Yuda Onsen parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563751}} on the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]]. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogori Interchange|1=Kogori Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463845}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]], or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Interchange|1=Yamaguchi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11044713}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 262|National Route 262]] and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōfu Higashi Interchange|1=Hofu-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657010}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.

Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yuda Onsen]][[ja:湯田温泉]][[zh:湯田溫泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563762]] Wikidata name: Yuda Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|Yuda Onsen in Yamaguchi Prefecture|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]|Yuda Onsen Gorge|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen|qq=}}, the former name of [[:en:Kindaichi Onsen|Kindaichi Onsen]] in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]|Kindaichi Onsen|{{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen|qq=}}, the former name of {{ill|Miyanoshiro Onsen|ja|宮之城温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanoshiro Onsen|1=Miyanoshiro Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453232}} in [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]|Miyanoshiro Onsen}}
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]] | Location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station]] }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Shiyu|1=Bōchō Shiyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65291872}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * [[:en:Alkali|Alkaline]] {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source Water Temperature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansuien|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koroshi Isamu|1=Isamu Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643116}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], the hot spring town features large [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]], and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|1=Tsuwano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146437}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/restaurants|1=restaurant|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666766}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|1=Kōfu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228449}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|1=Tottori Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674516}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|1=Matsue-shinjiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531630}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Osamu|1=Michio Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585906}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]]|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|qq=}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|1=Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466617}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-yama|1=Gongen-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542316}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō†Jizō|qq=}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as [[:en:Inoue Kaoru|Inoue Kaoru]] and [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Nakahara Chuya]]. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the [[:en:Nakahara Chūya Memorial Museum|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum]]. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuraya|qq=}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet [[:en:Santōka Taneda|Taneda Santoka]] once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpo no Yado|1=Kanpo no Yado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264563}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}}’s [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]] at [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]], about a 10-minute walk. === Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|1=Yamaguchi Prefectural Road Route 204|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467194}} (formerly [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]]), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Chūgoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JRバス中国|1=Chugoku JR Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10874403}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagi Express|1=Hagi Express|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620283}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|1=Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592158}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Shūnan route) === By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|1=Yuda Onsen parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563751}} on the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]]. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogori Interchange|1=Kogori Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463845}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]], or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Interchange|1=Yamaguchi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11044713}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 262|National Route 262]] and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōfu Higashi Interchange|1=Hofu-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657010}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=onsen banzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Rankings|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Rankings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562960}} * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近鉄グループホールディングス|1=Kintetsu Group Holdings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19758662}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | image = | Comment = White fox statue beside [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]] | Location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] {{Location map many | Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 34.163667 | lon_deg = 131.456722 | pos = bottom | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | lat2_deg = 34.172801 | lon2_deg = 131.480986 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station]] }}<center>Map of Yamaguchi Prefecture</center> | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 9 | Latitude Seconds = 52 | N (Northern Latitude/Southern Latitude) = N | Longitude Degrees = 131 | Longitude Minutes = 27 | Longitude Seconds = 24 | E (Eastern Longitude/Western Longitude) = E | Access = For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|アルカリ性単純泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Spring Temperature = 64 - 73°C | pH = 9.14 | Flow Rate = | Classification of Liquid Nature = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Yamaguchi | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 9 | lat_sec = 52 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 131 | lon_min = 27 | lon_sec = 24 | lon_dir = E | label = Yuda Onsen (湯田温泉) | width = 250 | caption = Location of Yuda Onsen | alt = Location of Yuda Onsen }} [[File:Onsen-dori Street in Yuda Hot Spring Area.jpg|thumb|250px|Onsen Street]] '''Yuda Onsen''' (ゆだおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]). It is one of the representative hot springs of Yamaguchi Prefecture (one of the "{{ill|Bōchō Shiyu|ja|防長四湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Shiyu|1=Bōchō Shiyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65291872}}").
== Spring Quality == 【Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring】 * [[:en:Alkali|Alkaline]] {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|性単純泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Source Water Temperature|ja|源泉温度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source Water Temperature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 63.6°C Many bathing facilities use a "Yuda Onsen Mixed Spring" produced by blending water from multiple sources, though some facilities such as {{ill|Sansuien|ja|山水園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansuien|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Umenoya use their own source water. Hot spring critic {{ill|Koroshi Isamu|ja|郡司勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koroshi Isamu|1=Isamu Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643116}} praised the water at Sansuien, stating, "It is an excellent hot spring. (...) The hot spring water of Yuda is renowned." <ref>[https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/80460/ "Famed Hot Spring – Bathing at Sansuien in Yuda Onsen; Shimizu Onsen too – Yamaguchi Prefecture’s best uncirculated hot spring, No.6"]</ref>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park)]] Located in the southwestern part of [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], the hot spring town features large [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]], and business hotels. Although few hot spring towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the foremost among them on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging hub for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as the famous sightseeing areas of [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|1=Tsuwano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146437}}, with an extensive bus network.
The area is also Yamaguchi City's central entertainment and commercial district, boasting many {{ill|restaurants|ja|飲食店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/restaurants|1=restaurant|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666766}}. Unlike hot springs that emerge in the heart of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|1=Kōfu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228449}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|1=Tottori Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674516}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|1=Matsue-shinjiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531630}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], the onsen here rises within a densely built-up city center. Although the atmosphere is distinct from that of a traditional hot spring town, there remain several spots where overnight guests can enjoy nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the hot spring town is reputed as a lodging place where late Edo period patriots once gathered.<ref>{{ill|Ishikawa Osamu|ja|石川理夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Osamu|1=Michio Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585906}}『温泉の日本史』 (Chūkō Shinsho, 2018), pp. 181–182</ref> Additionally, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}} as one of "10 inns to savor gourmet cuisine"<ref>{{ill|Ishii Hiroko|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko|1=Hiroko Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584925}}『感動の温泉宿100』 (Bunshun Shinsho, 2018)</ref> and was also listed in "200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths" by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}.<ref>{{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|1=Tadanori Matsuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532059}}『松田教授の“温泉道”』 (Chūsei Shuppan, 2008)</ref>.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five such facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Yuda Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on the foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility named "Fox's Footprints" offering foot baths.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/site/topics/2564.html|title=Opening Ceremony for Yuda Onsen Tourist Circulation Facility “Fox's Footprints”|website=Yamaguchi City Official Site|date=2016-09-01|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
Within the Yuda Onsen town, the only communal bath was "Kamenoyu" (which closed in 2020); other facilities include day-use baths and in-house bathing facilities attached to ryokan. Additionally, "Tennen Onsen Shimizuyu" located a little northeast in Chūō 4-chōme utilizes its own source water from the Yuda Onsen system as a public bath.
An arch-shaped sign reading "Welcome to Yamaguchi, Greetings from Yuda Onsen" once stood on the western side of the town, but due to deterioration, it was slated for removal in fiscal 2024 (Reiwa 6), with plans by the prefecture to install an electronic sign instead.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240906/k00/00m/040/067000c |title=Large Sign “Welcome to Yamaguchi” to be Removed – Over 20 Years Old |publisher=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2024-09-06 |accessdate=2024-09-06}}</ref>
=== Community Facilities === Yamaguchi City plans to establish a community and health promotion facility, the "Yuda Onsen Park & Exchange Center." Its nickname was decided as "Konkon Park" in March 2024 (Reiwa 6), with an opening scheduled for April 2025 (Reiwa 7). The facility will utilize the existing source water of Yuda Onsen to significantly reduce the energy consumption for bathing and heating equipment.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|author=Yamaguchi City|url=https://www.env.go.jp/content/000101194.pdf|title=“Zero Carbon Central City” – Achieving Urban Decarbonization through Co-creation among Shopping Streets, Residents, Businesses, and the City|publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]]|accessdate=2023-02-11}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASS3V3R16S3VTZNB004M.html|title=Yuda Onsen Park in Yamaguchi City, Nicknamed “Konkon Park”|date=2024-03-27|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
=== Festivals and Events === * {{ill|Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|ja|湯田温泉白狐まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen White Fox Festival|qq=}} (First weekend of April) * Yuda Chōchin Festival (August 6–7) ** In conjunction with the {{ill|Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|ja|山口七夕ちょうちんまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|1=Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466617}}, bamboo lanterns are displayed along Yunomachi-dōri and Nishikawa-dōri. On certain nights, both streets are closed to traffic and transformed into pedestrian paradises.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://y-chouchin.jp/event/湯田温泉/|title=Yuda Onsen|website=Official Site of Yamaguchi Tanabata Chōchin Festival|accessdate=2021-01-12}}</ref>
{{Expand section}}
== History == The hot spring is said to have been opened approximately 600 years ago. According to legend, a white fox was seen bathing in the hot spring every night, and it was discovered by a master from a temple at the foot of {{ill|Gongen-yama|ja|権現山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-yama|1=Gongen-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542316}}. The appearance of a golden {{ill|Jizō†Jizō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō†Jizō|qq=}} statue in the middle of a rice field, together with the emergence of the source water, is said to be the origin of the name "Yuda." In the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], anti-Imperial loyalist and court nobles frequented the area.
It is also the birthplace of notable figures such as [[:en:Inoue Kaoru|Inoue Kaoru]] and [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Nakahara Chuya]]. In Yamaguchi City, {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|1=Inoue Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16277560}} (formerly Takada Park) and Nakahara Hospital, both situated on extensive grounds in the hot spring town, were once the family homes of these individuals. Nakahara Hospital, however, was largely destroyed in a fire on May 6, 1972 (Shōwa 47), leaving only an Ibuki tree, a tea room, and a storehouse in front of the entrance. On February 18, 1994 (Heisei 6), a portion of the former family estate was redeveloped as the [[:en:Nakahara Chūya Memorial Museum|Nakahara Chuya Memorial Museum]]. Directly across from it, the ryokan {{ill|Nishimuraya|ja|西村屋 (湯田温泉)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuraya|qq=}}—where Chuya is said to have held his wedding ceremony—went bankrupt in 2017.
In addition, the famous haiku poet [[:en:Santōka Taneda|Taneda Santoka]] once resided in a small 4.5-tatami room adjacent to Ryūsenzan Temple in Yuda-mae Town, earning the nickname "Fūraikyo." He is said to have adored this hot spring; a stone monument bearing his representative haiku, “Chinpoko mo ososomo waite afureru yu” ("Even penises, slowly oozing, overflow with hot spring water"), stands here.<ref>[https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000146/card749.html "Aozora Bunko: Kusokutō"]</ref><ref>[http://www.yudaonsen.com/outline/bungaku.html Yuda Onsen Overview – Yuda Onsen Toramaki] – Yuda Onsen Ryokan Cooperative</ref>
The former {{ill|Kanpo no Yado|ja|かんぽの宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpo no Yado|1=Kanpo no Yado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264563}} Yuda, once located in Yuda Onsen, closed in December 2019 (Reiwa 1). Its site now houses the "Wellness Center Chūō," a comprehensive health management facility with hot spring amenities that opened on November 1, 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/127045|title=Former Kanpo no Yado Yuda Site to Become a Welfare Facility; Hot Spring and Day Service Scheduled for July 2022|date=2021-12-06|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sunday-yamaguchi.co.jp/cms/news/221029-news3-2|title=Comprehensive Health Management Facility Utilizing DX and Hot Spring Amenities “Wellness Center Chūō” to Open on November 1|date=2022-11-01|publisher=Sunday Yamaguchi|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
The impact of the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルスの影響|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} saw the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" suspend operations at the end of April 2020 (Reiwa 2), and the retirement facility "Suizan-sō" ceased operations at the end of January 2021 (Reiwa 3), with one additional ryokan also closing.<ref name="asahi20221123">{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASQCQ7DD6QCBTZNB01Q.html|title=Post-COVID, Yamaguchi City’s Yuda Onsen Sees a Series of Hotel and Ryokan Closures|date=2022-11-23|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref> Among these, the former "Plaza Hotel Kotobukisu" was acquired by Hotel Active (Yamaguchi City) in September 2020, and demolition of its five-story building commenced the following month, with a revival effort underway supported by Yamaguchi City and the Japan Tourism Agency.<ref name="asahi20221123" /> Moreover, the state-owned retirement facility "Suizan-sō" is slated for a new hot spring ryokan development project by a consortium led by Ban Kikaku (from Shūnan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture) and a group from Onna Juku (Tokyo) in 2024 (Reiwa 6), which has secured priority negotiation rights for its sale.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chugoku-np.co.jp/articles/-/462070|title=Suizan-sō in Yuda Onsen to be Sold to Ban Kikaku and Onna Juku, New Hot Spring Ryokan Planned – Yamaguchi Prefecture|date=2024-05-08|publisher=Chūgoku Shimbun|accessdate=2024-05-09}}</ref>
== Access == === Rail === The nearest station is {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}}’s [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]] at [[:en:Yudaonsen Station|Yuda Onsen Station]], about a 10-minute walk. === Bus === The hot spring district is traversed by {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|ja|山口県道204号宮野大歳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Road 204 Miyano Ōtose Line|1=Yamaguchi Prefectural Road Route 204|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467194}} (formerly [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]]), with numerous route buses operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} and {{ill|JR Bus Chūgoku|ja|中国JRバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Chūgoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JRバス中国|1=Chugoku JR Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10874403}}. The central bus stop, called "Yuda Onsen," (referred to as "Yuda Onsen-dōri" by JR Bus Chūgoku) sees highway buses departing and arriving from various directions. Highway buses stopping at "Yuda Onsen" include: * For Tokyo ({{ill|Hagi Express|ja|萩エクスプレス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagi Express|1=Hagi Express|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620283}}) * For Fukuoka/Hakata ({{ill|Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|ja|福岡・山口ライナー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|1=Fukuoka–Yamaguchi Liner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592158}}) * For Hiroshima (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Hiroshima Line) * For Shūnan (operated by {{ill|Bōchō Kōtsū|ja|防長交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōchō Kōtsū|1=Bocho Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657121}} on the Shūnan route) === By Car === The nearest parking area is the {{ill|Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|ja|湯田温泉パーキングエリア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda Onsen Smart Interchange|1=Yuda Onsen parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563751}} on the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]]. For non-ETC vehicles, one may use the {{ill|Kogori Interchange|ja|小郡インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogori Interchange|1=Kogori Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463845}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 9|National Route 9]], or from the {{ill|Yamaguchi Interchange|ja|山口インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Interchange|1=Yamaguchi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11044713}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 262|National Route 262]] and National Route 9. Additionally, vehicles from the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] can also access via the {{ill|Bōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōfu Higashi Interchange|1=Hofu-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657010}} via National Route 262 and National Route 9.
== Related Topics == {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: 温泉]]}} * [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], {{ill|Public Bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}}, {{ill|Hot Spring Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=onsen banzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Rankings|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Rankings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562960}} * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Kintetsu Group|ja|近鉄グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近鉄グループホールディングス|1=Kintetsu Group Holdings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19758662}} – Until March 31, 2015, a company by the name "Yuda Onsen Co., Ltd." operated Hotel Matsumasa as a corporate entity; the hotel was later transferred to {{ill|Yūber|ja|ユウベル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2015/0116/11p.html Yuda Onsen: Hotel Matsumasa, Business Transfer to U.I. Belle Moni] – Yamaguchi Shimbun, January 16, 2015.</ref>
